heroHeadline: PLA Surges to 28 Sorties on Apr 26 — New Non-Exercise Peak; 18 Enter Multi-Sector ADIZ; 8 PLAN Vessels; 18 Days to Trump-Xi Summit
heroSubtitle: Taiwan's MND detected 28 PLA aircraft sorties and 8 PLAN vessels on April 26, 2026 — a dramatic 3.5× single-day escalation from 8 sorties on April 25, and the highest non-exercise sortie count since Justice Mission-2025 (December 2025). Of the 28 sorties, 18 entered Taiwan's northern, central, and southwestern ADIZ simultaneously — a multi-sector incursion geometry matching the April 21 surge pattern, signaling a deliberate escalation of operational scope. The April 26 surge breaks the moderate oscillating pattern of April 22-25 and sets a new non-exercise peak for the pre-Trump-Xi summit period. Concurrently, Taiwan Deputy Foreign Minister Francois Wu publicly expressed Taipei's deepest fear: 'What we are the most afraid is to put Taiwan on the menu of the talk between Xi Jinping and President Trump' (Bloomberg/Taipei Times). Taiwan's 14-day Han Kuang 42 computer-assisted wargame concluded April 25, testing US-style military rehearsals and incorporating overseas conflict scenarios. The US House FY2027 appropriations bill (April 24) earmarks $500M in Foreign Military Financing for Taiwan. With 18 days to the May 14-15 Trump-Xi Beijing summit — and Beijing expected to press Trump for arms sale restraint and opposition to Taiwan independence — the 28-sortie surge signals PLA's pre-summit pressure campaign has entered its acute escalation phase.
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, DSCA, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, AIT, Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, CNBC, NPR, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, Nikkei Asia, Japan Times, ANI News, Tribune India, Vision Times, South China Morning Post, Taiwan News, Asia Times, The Asia Cable, GlobalSecurity.org, Xinhua, CGTN, The Diplomat, Washington Times, Global Times, People's Daily Online, Al Jazeera, The Manila Times, CNBC, MarineLink, Stars and Stripes, US News & World Report, Free Malaysia Today, Military Times, Foundation for Defense of Democracies, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
